title: cytisus hirsutus var. subleiocarpus
intro: Cytisus hirsutus var. subleiocarpus, also known as Scotch broom, is a shrub-like legume native to Europe and Western Asia, characterized by its yellow flowers and spiny branches. The plant is used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, which are attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and alkaloids. The health benefits of Scotch broom include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health, as well as its traditional use as a diuretic and expectorant. In herbal preparations, Scotch broom is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its use should be cautious due to potential side effects and interactions with medications.
